title: [InstCombine] Fold icmp over select of cmp more aggressively
summary: The generalization expands a pattern that folds `icmp (select cond, X, Y), C` into `select cond, icmp(X, C), icmp(Y, C)` by recognizing when either operand simplifies, not just when it's a constant. The key insight is that `llvm.ucmp`/`llvm.scmp` intrinsics with a constant RHS will fold to a simpler `icmp`, making the transformation profitable even if only one side is a constant. The patch introduces a helper `Simplifies` that checks for either a constant operand (`Op`) or a single-use cmp intrinsic with a constant RHS, enabling the fold when at least one side reduces. This allows the optimization to handle swapped select operands, cmp intrinsics, and non-constant RHS cases, while preserving the one-use safety check to avoid code growth. Developers should identify similar intrinsic-to-canonical folds to broaden pattern applicability.
